name: agent_task_data_store
description: Store task-specific data (code snippets, API responses, research findings) in agent memory with strict categorization and metadata. This function enforces proper data structure for reliable task data management and retrieval.

task_id.description: The task ID this data is associated with. Must match the current task being worked on.

data_key.description: A descriptive key/name for this data (e.g., 'github_api_response', 'code_snippet_main_function', 'research_findings'). Use snake_case for consistency.

data_content.description: The actual data content to store (code, text, JSON, etc.). Can be any text-based information.

data_type.description: The type of data being stored for proper categorization and retrieval.

current_step.description: The current step in the task where this data was gathered (e.g., 'code_analysis', 'api_research', 'debugging').

future_use.description: Brief explanation of how this data will be useful in future steps (e.g., 'will need this API response for creating the PR', 'this code pattern will be reused in similar functions').

priority.description: Priority level of this data for future retrieval. Use 'critical' for essential data, 'high' for important context, 'medium' for helpful info, 'low' for reference material.

metadata.description: Additional metadata for this data entry (optional).

size.description: Size of the data content in characters

parameters.data_content: {"id": 123, "title": "Fix login bug", "body": "The login function has a validation issue...", "state": "open"}
parameters.future_use: Will use this issue data when creating the fix and updating the issue status
This is the main function that needs to be fixed, will reference it when implementing the solution

parameters.data_content: Found 3 similar issues: #42 (authentication), #67 (validation), #89 (error handling). Pattern: all involve missing input validation.
parameters.future_use: Will use this pattern analysis to ensure comprehensive fix
